buggy_code:
import pandas as pd

# Load point-in-time universe — includes delisted stocks
universe = pd.read_csv('universe_history.csv', parse_dates=['date', 'delist_date'])

def get_universe_at_date(as_of_date):
    """
    Returns stocks that were active on a given date.
    Includes stocks that were later delisted — no survivorship bias.
    """
    active = universe[
        (universe['date'] <= as_of_date) &
        ((universe['delist_date'].isna()) | (universe['delist_date'] > as_of_date))
    ]
    return active['symbol'].tolist()

# Get universe as of a specific date
symbols_2020 = get_universe_at_date(pd.Timestamp('2020-01-01'))
description: Clean example — point-in-time universe that includes delisted stocks, avoiding survivorship bias.
explanation: The universe is filtered by both entry date and delist date. Stocks that were delisted after as_of_date are included. Stocks delisted before as_of_date are excluded. This correctly models what a trader would have known at that point in time.
